extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. The only transition that sets sent := true is t_send_allowed, whose guard is (not opted_out); thus sent can only become true in a state where opted_out is false, yielding the post-state opted_out=false, sent=true, which satisfies the implication (antecedent false). The only transition setting opted_out := true is t_optout, which simultaneously sets sent := false, so entering the opted_out state always leaves sent false. No transition ever clears opted_out back to false (no event resets it), so opted_out is monotonic. While opted_out is true, every applicable event keeps the property: optout sets sent:=false; send_attempt takes t_send_blocked (since t_send_allowed's guard not opted_out is false) setting sent:=false; tick sets sent:=false. There is no reachable state with opted_out=true and sent=true. The initial state has opted_out=false (antecedent false). Therefore the property is never violated, including within the 6-event bound.
